prompt: Should I buy Banque de Tunisie (BT) stock given the new foreign-currency rules?

event.summary: BT P/E 7.4, ROE 12.1%, div yield 5.2%. Balance sheet: T1 ratio 11.8%. Stable.
Dec 2025: residents now allowed FX accounts → tailwind for BT retail FX fee income.
Beta 0.62 vs TUNINDEX. 30-day VaR at 95%: −4.1%. Correlated with BNA and BIAT.
All three findings are grounded. No speculative claim detected. Approved.

final: On balance, BT looks fundamentally sound [fundamentals] and the new FX-account rule is a genuine tailwind for retail FX revenue [news]. Downside risk is moderate and typical for Tunisian banks [risk]. This is educational — not personalized advice. A reasonable next step is to model a small position (e.g. 2–5% of portfolio) and compare against BIAT or ATB before committing.
